drakantosReleaseDateAndBetaAccess.title: Drakantos Release Date and Beta Access
drakantosReleaseDateAndBetaAccess.intro: Drakantos is a free-to-play pixel-art action MMORPG from Wingeon Game Studios. The public Steam release is not live yet, but players can request playtest access on Steam, and the latest closed beta runs from May 22 to May 24, 2026, starting at 9 AM ET on Friday.

items.title: Check the Steam release status
items.description: The Steam store page lists Drakantos as Coming soon, so the full public release is not available yet.
items.action: Wishlist the game and use the Steam Playtest request button.

items.title: Request Steam Playtest access
items.description: Open the Drakantos Steam page and press Request Access. Steam will notify selected players when the developer is ready to add more participants.

items.title: Join the May closed beta
items.description: The May closed beta runs from May 22 to May 24, 2026, starting at 9 AM ET on Friday.
items.action: Launch through Steam after access is granted.

items.title: Keep previous beta access
items.description: Players who received access during the previous beta keep access and do not need to request it again.
items.action: Use the same Steam account that already has access.

items.title: Invite up to three friends
items.description: Players with beta access can invite up to 3 friends, as long as those Steam friendships are at least 30 days old.
items.action: Invite eligible Steam friends after your access is active.

items.title: Choose a server region carefully
items.description: The beta includes NA, SA, EU, and AS server regions. Once a region is selected for this test, it cannot be changed during the beta.
items.action: Pick the region closest to where you want to play.

items.title: Expect queues during scaling
items.description: The beta can use a queue system while the team scales server capacity and gradually allows more players online.
items.action: Wait in queue if the servers are under load.

items.title: Plan around the beta wipe
items.description: All progress made during the beta test will be wiped before the full release.
items.action: Treat the beta as testing, not permanent progression.

drakantosCodesAndRewards.title: Drakantos Codes and Rewards
drakantosCodesAndRewards.intro: Drakantos does not currently work like a permanent redeem-code page. During the closed beta, the key player rewards come from Steam Playtest access, Twitch account linking, PvP and PvE beta activity, and creator or friend invite routes.

items.label: Current Code Status
items.reward: No permanent typed redeem-code list is listed in the official beta announcements.
items.how_to_claim: Use the Steam Playtest request, Twitch linking, and beta activities instead.

items.label: Steam Playtest Access
items.reward: Closed beta access through the Steam Playtest system.
items.how_to_claim: Press Request Access on the Drakantos Steam page and wait for Steam notification.

items.label: Friend Invite Access
items.reward: Players with beta access can invite up to 3 eligible Steam friends.
items.how_to_claim: Use a Steam friend connection that has existed for at least 30 days.

items.label: Twitch Drops
items.reward: Temporary closed-beta rewards from watching channels streaming in the Drakantos category.
items.how_to_claim: After downloading the game, go to Settings > Account > Twitch and link your Twitch account.

items.label: Beta Boxes
items.reward: Orb Box, Artifact Box, and Trophy Box rewards let players choose 1 item from 3 options.
items.how_to_claim: Earn Beta Boxes through PvP and PvE activities during the closed beta.

items.label: Creator Beta Keys
items.reward: Content creators can apply through the Drakantos creator route for beta-related access opportunities.
items.how_to_claim: Use the Drakantos Creator Program link shared in the official announcements.

drakantosBeginnerGuide.title: Drakantos Beginner Guide
drakantosBeginnerGuide.intro: Drakantos replaces traditional custom classes with hero-based characters. During beta, new players can test early progression, PvE content, PvP systems, shared account progression, and build customization through orbs, artifacts, trophies, and temporary beta rewards.

items.title: Request access first
items.description: Go to the Steam page and press Request Access for the Drakantos Playtest. Selected players receive access through Steam, and previous beta access remains active.
items.player_tip: Use the same Steam account you want to play on.

items.title: Download and pick your region
items.description: After access is granted, download the game through Steam and select a beta region. Available regions are NA, SA, EU, and AS, and the region cannot be changed during the test.
items.player_tip: Choose the region where your friends will play.

items.title: Choose a hero instead of a custom class
items.description: The beta gives all players access to 15 heroes from the start: Liam, Marly, Zeugladius, Reya, Yura, Ojore, Thomas, Byron, Iris, Ozul, Faendar, Arryn, Orbryn, Yuki, and Korz.
items.player_tip: Try multiple heroes early because Drakantos is built around hero swapping.

items.title: Understand hero progression
items.description: Hero level and gear do not carry over between heroes, but there is account-wide cross-progression. Artifacts, trophies, their upgrades, mounts, pets, and cosmetics are shared across the account.
items.player_tip: You can build a pool of heroes for different bosses, missions, and PvP modes.

items.title: Learn the action combat controls
items.description: The current combat system is fully action-based with no target system. Left click is the basic attack, right click is the special attack, skills are aimed with the mouse, and the character faces the mouse direction when attacking.
items.player_tip: Use mouse aim for attacks and expect the game to feel different from the earlier tile-based beta.

items.title: Use useful beta inputs
items.description: Press the mouse wheel to open the ping wheel, press Tab to open the scoreboard, and press Ctrl + R to report bugs directly in-game.
items.player_tip: These shortcuts are useful in PvP testing and group play.

items.title: Choose PvE, PvP, or both
items.description: The beta includes PvE content from the previous beta and a PvP-focused test with Arena maps and modes. PvP players can test Control, Escort, and Assault-style objectives, while PvE players can still test early progression and gameplay changes.
items.player_tip: Use PvP and PvE activities to earn Beta Boxes during the test.

items.title: Treat beta progress as temporary
items.description: All beta progress and temporary beta reward items reset after the test, including closed-beta Twitch rewards.
items.player_tip: Use the beta to learn heroes, controls, and builds rather than to save permanent progress.

drakantosHeroesGuide.title: Drakantos Heroes Guide
drakantosHeroesGuide.intro: The May closed beta gives all players 15 heroes from the start. Drakantos also lists more than 20 playable heroes overall, with hero swapping, shared account systems, orbs, artifacts, and trophies creating different build paths for each character.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A straightforward hero choice for players who want a classic armored fighter style within Drakantos hero-based progression.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A support-focused priest hero built around healing, holy shields, AoE attacks, and defensive utility.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A paladin-style hero for players who want a durable holy warrior angle with hero-specific abilities and lore.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A lightning-themed hero suited to players who want fast action combat and storm-flavored damage tools.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A sword-focused hero for players who want a wandering warrior fantasy and precise close-range gameplay.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: An earth-themed hero for players who like heavy-impact combat and disruptive battlefield presence.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A bard cat hero specialized in support flavor, music magic, team utility, and a distinctive personality angle.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A mage-style hero for players who want a spellcaster identity and a more traditional fantasy magic role.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: An illusion-themed hero for players who want a trickier magical playstyle and character-specific mechanics.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A rogue archetype for players who want an offensive specialist feel and quick damage-focused gameplay.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A druid-style hero for players who want a nature-magic fantasy and flexible RPG build customization.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A ranged archer-style hero for players who prefer projectile combat and positioning-based gameplay.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A gadget-focused hunter hero for players who like tactical tools, ranged pressure, and mechanical flavor.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A tank hero that relies on agility, mobility, evasion-style damage tools, ice powers, and crowd control.

items.availability: Playable from the start in the May Closed Beta
items.gameplay_note: A berserker-style hero for players who want an aggressive melee identity and high-pressure combat fantasy.

drakantosHeroTierList.title: Drakantos Hero Tier List
drakantosHeroTierList.intro: Drakantos uses a hero-based class system where each hero has unique abilities, lore, and personality. During the closed beta, 15 heroes are available from the start, and the current best approach is to choose by role, comfort, and build options rather than chasing one fixed damage meta.

heroes.name: Yuki
heroes.summary: Yuki is a mobile tank-style kitsune who uses agility, evasion tools, ice powers, and strong crowd control. Early beta discussion also highlighted her AoE clearing as comfortable for dungeon runs.

heroes.name: Marly
heroes.summary: Marly is a priest-style hero focused on healing, holy shields, and AoE attacks. She is a strong pick for players who want team value without giving up active combat.

heroes.name: Liam
heroes.summary: Liam is a knight-style hero with defensive value. Community Q&A notes describe his ultimate as a defensive tool with damage reduction and invulnerability during the animation.

heroes.name: Zeugladius
heroes.summary: Zeugladius is part of the confirmed 15-hero beta roster and is best evaluated through the new action-combat system, orbs, artifacts, and trophies.

heroes.name: Reya
heroes.summary: Reya is part of the confirmed beta roster. Use her to test how different hero kits feel under free movement and mouse-aimed combat.

heroes.name: Yura
heroes.summary: Yura is part of the confirmed beta roster and fits the account-wide hero system where players can unlock and swap heroes instead of creating new characters.

heroes.name: Ojore
heroes.summary: Ojore is part of the confirmed beta roster and can be tested with shared account systems such as artifacts, trophies, cosmetics, mounts, and pets.

heroes.name: Thomas
heroes.summary: Thomas is part of the confirmed beta roster. Try him across PvE and Arena to compare how his kit performs in different objectives.

heroes.name: Byron
heroes.summary: Byron is part of the confirmed beta roster and should be tested with the updated full action-combat controls.

heroes.name: Iris
heroes.summary: Iris is part of the confirmed beta roster. She is useful for players who want to compare hero mechanics across missions and PvP modes.

heroes.name: Ozul
heroes.summary: Ozul is part of the confirmed beta roster and can be tested with beta boxes that unlock orbs, artifacts, and trophies.

heroes.name: Faendar
heroes.summary: Faendar is part of the confirmed beta roster and fits the game's multi-hero account progression design.

heroes.name: Arryn
heroes.summary: Arryn is part of the confirmed beta roster. Test different orb, artifact, and trophy combinations to shape the playstyle.

heroes.name: Orbryn
heroes.summary: Orbryn is part of the confirmed beta roster and can be used to compare hero performance across Arena maps and mission objectives.

heroes.name: Korz
heroes.summary: Korz is part of the confirmed beta roster and benefits from the same shared account systems as other heroes.

heroes.name: Ranged and aim-heavy picks
heroes.summary: Some early beta feedback found archer-style gameplay harder under the older movement feel. The newer beta uses free movement and mouse-aimed action combat, making ranged control a key area to retest.

drakantosBuildsAndSkillsGuide.title: Drakantos Builds and Skills Guide
drakantosBuildsAndSkillsGuide.intro: Builds in Drakantos are not limited to a single hero skill bar. A hero has a core kit, then orbs, artifacts, trophies, equipment, and account-wide progression give players more ways to change playstyle across PvE and PvP.

items.whatItChanges: Your main class identity, abilities, lore, and combat feel.
items.howToUse: Pick a hero based on the content you want to play, then swap heroes freely from your account instead of creating a new character.

items.whatItChanges: Hero ability behavior and skill customization.
items.howToUse: Use orbs to change how a hero's existing skills play. During beta, Orb Boxes can give hero-specific orb choices.

items.whatItChanges: Additional active skills that expand your combat options.
items.howToUse: Equip artifacts to add new active tools for damage, utility, defense, or PvP adaptation.

items.whatItChanges: Passive abilities that support your build direction.
items.howToUse: Use trophies to add passive effects that match your hero role, mission goal, or PvP strategy.
Level and gear are managed per hero, while later shared systems reduce repeated endgame farming.

items.whatItChanges: Account-wide systems such as artifacts, trophies, upgrades, mounts, pets, and cosmetics.
items.howToUse: Build a hero pool over time. Once one hero reaches the required level and power, endgame accessories become shared.

items.whatItChanges: Temporary beta access to orbs, artifacts, and trophies.
items.howToUse: Open Orb Boxes, Artifact Boxes, and Trophy Boxes to choose 1 item from 3 options and test different builds quickly.

drakantosDungeonsAndMissionsGuide.title: Drakantos Dungeons and Missions Guide
drakantosDungeonsAndMissionsGuide.intro: Drakantos combines open-world exploration with instanced mission and dungeon content from the Adventurers' Guild. Missions are built around changing objectives, enemies, bosses, rewards, and special events so each run can feel different.

items.title: Adventurers' Guild Mission Flow
items.content: Adventurers' Guild missions act as the main dungeon-style PvE loop. Players enter missions with main objectives, secondary objectives, matchmaking, ranks, and difficulty scaling.

items.title: Ranks and Difficulty
items.content: The beta lets players reach Rank 5 of the Adventurers' Guild, while the full game is planned up to Rank 14. Each rank introduces new enemies, mini-bosses, dungeon content, and harder challenges.

items.title: Boss Access
items.content: Drakantos has 16 major bosses listed for the broader game. These bosses normally start appearing from Rank 8 onward, but one major boss was tuned down for Rank 5 beta access.

items.title: Dynamic Mission Content
items.content: Missions can change over time with different enemies, bosses, resources, traps, objectives, and special events. This structure supports repeated dungeon runs without every run feeling identical.

items.title: Mini Bosses
items.content: Mini bosses are designed to make early levels and regular missions more dynamic. They add challenge before players reach the later endgame boss tiers.

items.title: Loot and Rewards
items.content: Loot tables cover enemies, missions, and bosses, including equipment, artifacts, orbs, cosmetics, and other rewards. Experience gains are also configured for enemies, missions, and bosses.

items.title: Adventure Score and MVP Bonus
items.content: Each monster kill and side objective increases end-of-adventure points, improving rewards and experience. The best-performing player in an Adventure receives a 10% extra points bonus.

items.title: Continue After Main Objective
items.content: Instead of ending automatically when the main objective is complete, an Adventure can continue so players can finish side objectives, defeat more monsters, and earn better rewards.

drakantosPvpGuide.title: Drakantos PvP Guide
drakantosPvpGuide.intro: Drakantos includes several PvP paths, from structured Arena matches to open-world duels and PvPvE encounters. The recent beta focuses heavily on PvP, free movement, mouse-aimed action combat, and equalized competitive systems.

items.title: Arena
items.summary: Arena is the main competitive PvP mode and is designed like a mini MOBA inside Drakantos. Matches use 3v3 teams, random map and mode selection, a hero selection phase, and equalized stats.

items.title: Control
items.summary: Teams fight over control zones. Standing on the zones earns progress, and the first team to fully capture all required zones wins.

items.title: Escort
items.summary: Each team escorts its own crystal to the final point while trying to stop the enemy crystal. The crystal moves when allies are nearby and stops when enemies contest it.

items.title: Assault
items.summary: Assault works like a MOBA mode with minions, towers, and a core. A center-map buff appears every 3:30 and grants 25% attack for 60 seconds.

items.title: Open-World Duels
items.summary: Players can send duel requests to other players in the open world. This is useful for testing skills, practicing matchups, and fighting friends outside formal Arena matches.

items.title: Red Chests
items.summary: Red chests trigger PvP-focused open-world encounters. After a short gathering window, players fight in a chaotic free-for-all, and the last player standing claims most of the rewards.

items.title: Adventure Invasions
items.summary: Adventure Invasions can place two groups into the same mission instance. Teams collect red Erilith crystals, fight demonic enemies, and can defeat enemy players to steal crystals.

items.title: Hellgates
items.summary: Hellgates combine PvE enemies, loot gathering, and PvP tension. Players enter solo or in groups, decide when to extract, and risk losing collected loot if they die.

items.title: PvP Controls
items.summary: Drakantos now uses full action combat with no target system. Left click is the basic attack, right click is the special attack, the mouse wheel opens the ping wheel, and TAB opens the scoreboard.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Drakantos

questions.question: Is Drakantos free to play on Steam?
questions.answer: Yes. Drakantos is listed as a free-to-play game on Steam, while access during test periods is managed through Steam Playtest waves.

questions.question: How do I get Drakantos beta access?
questions.answer: Request access from the Drakantos Steam page. Players with prior beta access keep access, and eligible players can invite up to three long-term Steam friends.

questions.question: How many heroes are playable in Drakantos beta?
questions.answer: The current closed beta provides 15 playable heroes from the start, and each hero has separate level and gear progression.

questions.question: Does Drakantos support PvE and PvP progression?
questions.answer: Yes. Drakantos supports Adventurers' Guild PvE missions and multiple PvP modes such as Arena, duels, and open-world events.

questions.question: How do Drakantos builds work?
questions.answer: Builds combine hero kits with orbs, artifacts, trophies, equipment, and shared account progression systems.

questions.question: Will Drakantos beta progress reset?
questions.answer: Yes. Closed beta progress and beta reward progression are reset before full release, so the beta should be treated as a testing phase.
